metric_explanations.why: Strict correctness score: model must output the exact expected answer.
metric_explanations.improve_effect: Higher value means fewer outright wrong final answers and better task reliability.

metric_explanations.why: Correctness after normalization (spacing/punctuation/case). Reduces formatting noise.
metric_explanations.improve_effect: Higher value means semantic correctness is improving even when formatting differs.

metric_explanations.why: For numeric tasks, checks exact numeric equality with ground truth.
metric_explanations.improve_effect: Higher value means stronger arithmetic precision and less numeric hallucination.

metric_explanations.why: Counts near-correct numeric outputs as correct within tolerance.
metric_explanations.improve_effect: Higher value means model is converging toward correct magnitudes, even with rounding noise.

metric_explanations.why: Average absolute distance from true numeric answers.
metric_explanations.improve_effect: Lower value means typical numeric mistakes are smaller and less harmful.

metric_explanations.why: Overlap quality between prediction text and reference text.
metric_explanations.improve_effect: Higher value means better answer content coverage, even when exact strings differ.
Higher value means outputs are structurally closer to targets.

metric_explanations.why: Measures how often model emits an extractable final answer.
metric_explanations.improve_effect: Higher value means better answer formatting and easier downstream evaluation.

metric_explanations.why: How probable the model thinks true answers are, token by token.
metric_explanations.improve_effect: Lower value means the model distribution aligns better with correct answers.

metric_explanations.why: Exponentiated NLL; interpretable confidence/calibration metric on correct answers.
metric_explanations.improve_effect: Lower value means model is less surprised by correct outputs, typically improving generation quality.
Lower (without losing accuracy) means more concise answers and lower inference cost.

metric_explanations.why: How often generation hit the max token cap.
metric_explanations.improve_effect: Lower value means fewer incomplete answers and better decoding control.
Lower value means faster user experience and higher serving efficiency.
Higher value means better deployment throughput and lower runtime cost per token.
